basics.name: Lloyd Sanderson
basics.label: Computer Science and Japanese Graduate
basics.summary: Computer Science and Japanese graduate with hands-on experience in software development, game development, artificial intelligence, procedural systems, and 3D content creation. Proficient in C++, C#, Python, Java, Unity, Unreal Engine, and Blender through individual and collaborative technical projects. Combines technical problem-solving, creative development, Japanese language proficiency, and cross-cultural communication.

projects.name: Project Reap
projects.description: Collaborative 2D action-platformer developed in Unity and set in a stylized post-war Japanese environment.
highlights: Collaborated with a multidisciplinary team to develop a functional game prototype.
Implemented player movement, combat interactions, enemy behavior, and a chain-based Flow ability.
Contributed to gameplay design, animation, asset integration, testing, and debugging.

projects.name: If You're Not First
projects.description: Checkpoint-based racing prototype featuring player-versus-AI competition, lap tracking, vehicle health, and boost mechanics.
highlights: Developed checkpoint, lap-order, and distance-comparison systems using object-oriented C#.
Implemented opponent AI, vehicle movement, health, and boost functionality.
Created and integrated Blender assets into a playable Unity WebGL project.

projects.name: Star Wars Question-Answering System
projects.description: Natural-language question-answering application built in Prolog using a Definite Clause Grammar and structured fact database.
highlights: Designed grammar rules capable of interpreting yes-or-no, who, and what questions.
Mapped natural-language input to semantic Prolog predicates and database queries.
Implemented structured answer formatting and contextual feedback for unsupported queries.

projects.name: Blender Command-Line Integration
projects.description: Cross-language automation system connecting a Common Lisp command-line client to a Python socket server embedded in Blender.
highlights: Designed a TCP socket interface for exchanging structured JSON commands.
Implemented commands for object creation, transformations, materials, lighting, cameras, particles, and animation.
Created an interactive Common Lisp client for remotely controlling Blender operations.
